title: Arrow character '→' in log string causes UnicodeEncodeError on Windows cp1252 console
description: The string `f"Switching branch: {current_branch} → {branch}"` uses the Unicode right-arrow character (U+2192). On Windows terminals with a cp1252 (or similar non-UTF-8) code page, `print()` raises `UnicodeEncodeError: 'charmap' codec can't encode character '\u2192'`. This is confirmed by the test suite: `test_switches_branch_when_needed` fails with exactly this error on the Windows Python 3.13 runner.
suggestedFix: Replace the arrow with an ASCII equivalent: `f"Switching branch: {current_branch} -> {branch}"`. Alternatively, set `PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8` in the shebang documentation, but the simpler and more robust fix is to avoid non-ASCII literals in log strings.

title: Double-log on dry-run for new-file path in copy_file_with_diff
description: In the 'new file' branch (lines 106–112), when `config.dry_run` is True the code logs `[DRY-RUN] Would add {label}` AND then unconditionally logs `add    {label}` on line 111. This produces two log lines for a single planned operation, incorrectly implying the file was actually added. The same pattern exists in `ensure_dir` (lines 131–135).
suggestedFix: In the new-file branch, move `log_info(_TAG, f"add    {label}")` inside the `else` block (i.e., only when `not config.dry_run`). Apply the same fix to `ensure_dir`: move `log_info(_TAG, f"create {label}/")` inside the `else` branch. The dry-run branch already logs `[DRY-RUN] Would add/create …` which is sufficient.

title: Double-log on dry-run for empty-directory removal in remove_local_files
description: In `remove_local_files` (lines 115–130), when `config.dry_run` is True the code logs `[DRY-RUN] Would remove {rel}/ (empty)` AND then unconditionally logs `remove {rel}/ (empty)` on the next line. This is the same double-log pattern as finding #2, confirmed by the test output showing both lines emitted during dry-run uninstall.
suggestedFix: Move `log_info(_TAG, f"remove {rel}/ (empty)")` inside the `else` branch so it only fires when `not config.dry_run`. The dry-run branch already logs the `[DRY-RUN]` line.

title: Double-log on dry-run for remove_file
description: In `remove_file` (lines 145–151), when `config.dry_run` is True the code logs `[DRY-RUN] Would remove {label}` AND then unconditionally logs `remove {label}` on line 150. Confirmed by test output: dry-run uninstall emits both lines for every file.
suggestedFix: Move `log_info(_TAG, f"remove {label}")` inside the `else` branch (only when `not config.dry_run`). The dry-run branch already logs `[DRY-RUN] Would remove …`.

title: Same '→' arrow character used in auto_fetch_source log line
description: `f"Switching source branch: {current_branch} → {config.branch}"` on line 136 has the same non-ASCII arrow character as finding #1. Will cause the same UnicodeEncodeError on Windows cp1252 consoles.
suggestedFix: Replace `→` with `->` for ASCII safety, consistent with the fix for finding #1.
